tasks_completed.description: Verified Item B: Write-guard sentinel three-candidate path exists, is internally consistent, and the CF-1 overwrite-only invariant holds. No functional gap found; no harden required (clean verify no-op).
tasks_completed.rationale: BA spec scoped Item B as verify-and-harden. All three paths confirmed present and consistent at the cited line ranges; no divergence to patch.

AC-B1_candidate_key_parity.pretool: hooks/pretool-write-guard.sh:79-85 — writer_key = env_task_id if env_task_id else session_id; enumerate [writer_key, data_task_id, session_id]; de-dup via seen-set preserving priority order.
AC-B1_candidate_key_parity.posttool: hooks/posttool-allowlist-consume.py:230-238 — writer_primary = env_task_id if env_task_id else session_id; enumerate [writer_primary, data_task_id, session_id]; de-dup via seen-set preserving priority order.
AC-B1_candidate_key_parity.conclusion: Writer-key derivation (CLAUDE_TASK_ID else session_id), enumeration order [writer_key, data.task_id, session_id], and de-dup semantics are IDENTICAL between pretool and posttool. Both invoke match_sentinel_grant_for_write(key, session_id, target_path) (allowlist.py:523-576) with the same argument order. No divergence.

AC-B2_CF1_overwrite_only.conclusion: CF-1 legacy-grant suppression (exit 2) is gated on [ -f "$FILE_PATH" ], firing ONLY for overwrite of an existing file. New-file creation does not satisfy the -f test and falls through to line 147 (exit 0), so an unrelated valid sentinel never blocks new-file creation. Invariant confirmed.

tests_run.suite: tests/generated/20260522-080646-B (AC1 matcher, AC2 write-guard sentinel check, AC3 posttool consume, AC4 scope integrity)
tests/generated/20260524-125300-B (AC1-AC8 write-scoped grant / wildcard / exploit-shape regression)

AC-B3_no_regression.pre_existing_failure_note: hooks/tests/test_allowlist_consolidation.py::test_regex_match FAILS with TypeError: 'NoneType' object cannot be interpreted as an integer at allowlist.py:59 (signal.alarm(timeout) when timeout is None). This is in the LEGACY regex-grant matching path (_match_loaded_grant / _regex_safe), NOT the Write-sentinel three-candidate chain. It is present on baseline f551a24 with zero edits from this task, hence pre-existing and out of Item B scope. Flagged for awareness only.

git_rationale.root_cause_commit: N/A — verification of already-landed Write-sentinel chain (tasks 20260522-080646-B, 20260524-125300-B)
git_rationale.why_issue_occurred: Item B was scoped as a parity-audit of an already-implemented three-path sentinel chain; the concern was potential pretool/posttool candidate-key divergence (grant found at write but not consumed = leak, or vice versa).
git_rationale.how_fix_addresses_root: No fix needed. Audit confirms candidate-key parity and the CF-1 overwrite-only invariant hold exactly as specified. The chain is internally consistent.

dev.qa_notes: Item B is a clean verify no-op (zero code changes). QA can re-confirm: (1) candidate-key list parity at write-guard.sh:79-85 vs consume.py:230-238 [writer_key, data_task_id, session_id]; (2) CF-1 -f gating at write-guard.sh:114; (3) re-run tests/generated/20260522-080646-B + 20260524-125300-B (expect 4+8 pass). The test_allowlist_consolidation.py::test_regex_match failure is pre-existing and unrelated to Item B.

self_verification.notes: py_compile + bash -n clean on all three target files; 12 Write-sentinel regression tests pass (4 + 8). All three AC-B criteria verified PASS by source inspection. No edits made, so no build/behavior change to introduce regressions.

implementation_notes: Item B required NO code change. The three-path Write-sentinel chain (allowlist.py:523-576 matcher, pretool-write-guard.sh:60-116 CF-1 check, posttool-allowlist-consume.py:222-272 consume) is already internally consistent: candidate-key derivation, enumeration order [writer_key, data_task_id, session_id], and de-dup are identical pretool<->posttool; CF-1 suppression is correctly gated on [ -f "$FILE_PATH" ] (overwrite-only). DO-NOT list respected: no touch to hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh or hooks/lib/bash_context_strip.py (Item A). pre_existing_guards (write-guard.sh:114, write-guard.sh:1143-equivalent, bash-safety:1096) were neither removed nor weakened (no edit). Pre-existing unrelated failure noted: test_allowlist_consolidation.py::test_regex_match (signal.alarm(None) in legacy regex path) — present on baseline, out of Item B scope, flagged for orchestrator awareness.
recommendations: Out of scope for Item B but worth a separate ticket: fix the pre-existing test_allowlist_consolidation.py::test_regex_match failure (pass a numeric default regex_timeout to _regex_safe / _match_loaded_grant instead of None, to avoid signal.alarm(None) TypeError in the legacy regex-grant path).
